body.u: It was a rainy day and the weather was stopping me from gardening. I leant on the window sill and gazed out of the window at the rain beating on the pavement. Instantly my memories came back.

At the age of sixteen and a half I went into the Royal Navy. 6 months training to see if I liked it. After doing this I signed on for tweelvetwelveyearsyears' service. In which I travelled around the world and visited many countries including America, Gibraltar and the PacifficPacific islands. After doing only tweelvetwelveyearsyears' service I signed for another 6 years and travelled some more. When I thought I had served the navy long enough I came out into Civi Street and got a job working in a factory as a machine Operator earning almost £80 a week which I needed to keep my wife who I had met in the navy as a WREN and also a couple of kids we now had.

As I got towards retirement the jobs became harder and more tiring and so the factory who employed me gave me easier jobs until I was not able to work anymore because of rhumatismrheumatism and other kinds of complaints.
For the past few years of my working life I was drawing money of social security. Until I drew my Old Age Pension at 63.
What an exitingexciting life it had been.
